info.title: VecDB Memory API
info.description:
Unified memory API at `/v1/memories/*`. Mode-free reads (list, search,
get-by-id), async-by-default ingest (`POST /v1/memories` → 202 + job_id,
or `?wait=true` for inline 200 within 30s), and polling at
`GET /v1/memories/jobs/{job_id}`.

## Authentication

Send your API key — the key is the sole identity:

- `Authorization: Token <api-key>` *or* `x-api-key: <api-key>`

The org is derived server-side from the key; you no longer send an org
id. Every row is scoped to the calling org; cross-org reads are not
possible even with a guessed id.

> **Deprecated:** `X-Org-Id` is still accepted during a compatibility
> window. If sent, it must match the key's org (a mismatch returns
> `403 org_mismatch`); it will be removed in a future release. New
> integrations must not send it.

**Optional diagnostic header.** You may send
`X-Xtrace-Client: <name>/<version>` (e.g. `xtrace-sdk/2.4.1`) on any
request. It never affects auth or behavior — it's used only to measure
client-version adoption. Absence is fine.

## Ingest contract (`POST /v1/memories`)

```
{
  "messages":          [...],     // REQUIRED — chat-style turns
  "user_id":           "alice",   // REQUIRED — per-user scope
  "conv_id":           "conv-1",  // REQUIRED — conversation anchor
  "agent_id":          "...",     // optional
  "app_id":            "...",     // optional
  "timestamp_format":  "%Y-%m-%d", // optional — strptime fmt for dated batch turns
  "extract_artifacts": false,     // opt-in to artifact-extraction stage
  "group_ids":         []         // optional — see "Group tagging" below
}
```

- **Required entities:** `user_id` and `conv_id`. `user_id` keys the
  per-user namespace (facts accumulate across that user's
  conversations); `conv_id` anchors each extracted memory to a session
  for replay, export, and bulk retract.
- Facts are always extracted from messages. Episodes are always
  extracted (`conv_id` is guaranteed). Artifacts are extracted only
  when `extract_artifacts: true` is set.
- The server picks live vs batch extraction by message count; no
  client-facing pipeline hint exists.
- Returns `202 Accepted` + `IngestJobResponse` with `status: "pending"`
  by default. With `?wait=true`, the server holds the connection up to
  ~30s and returns `200 OK` + terminal `IngestJobResponse` if
  extraction completes within the window; otherwise falls back to 202.

## Group tagging

Groups are client-registered tagging targets. A group MAY carry a
`prompt` describing what belongs in it; the ingest classifier reads
each prompt and decides — per extracted memory — which groups (if any)
to tag the row with. A group WITHOUT a prompt is a **catch-all**:
every extracted memory judged shareable is tagged with it, no
per-group matching. The tags become a filterable axis on search and
list, so other users (e.g. participants in the same trip) can find
each other's tagged memories without needing direct access to the
authoring user.

1. **Register groups** via `POST /v1/groups` (one-time per group).
   Returns an opaque `grp_<32-hex>` id. Omit `prompt` (or send null)
   to register a catch-all group; an empty string is rejected (422).
2. **Pass ids on ingest** in `IngestRequest.group_ids`. Cap is
   `MAX_GROUP_IDS_PER_INGEST` (default 20) — over-cap → `422
   invalid_request`.
3. **Unknown / archived ids are dropped silently** and echoed back in
   `IngestResult.ignored_group_ids`. A single stale id never fails an
   ingest — stale-cache self-correction is the deliberate model.
4. **Personal memories are never group-tagged.** The classifier
   judges every extracted memory personal-vs-shareable first
   (personal ≈ private circumstances: health, family matters,
   finances, credentials — not merely "about the user"). Personal
   rows stay in the authoring user's personal scope and are excluded
   from ALL groups, prompted and catch-all alike. If the verdict is
   unavailable the row is left untagged (fails closed).
5. **Tags are stamped per-item.** Each shareable fact / artifact /
   episode gets the prompted groups its content matches plus every
   requested catch-all group. A row may end up with zero tags even
   when several `group_ids` were sent.
6. **`group_ids` is frozen at ingest.** `PATCH /v1/memories/{id}`
   rejects attempts to set it (`422 immutable_field`). Edits to a
   group's `prompt` do NOT retroactively re-tag historical rows; a
   prompt can be added to a catch-all group later (it stops being a
   catch-all going forward) but never removed.

### Filtering by group on search

```
POST /v1/memories/search
{
  "query":     "what's the plan for this trip?",
  "user_id":   "bob",
  "group_ids": ["grp_tokyo_2026", "grp_paris_2027"]
}
```

When `group_ids` is non-empty, the read scope is the **union** of the
caller's own memories and everything tagged to the requested groups:

```
org
  AND ( user_id == <user_id>  OR  group_ids ∩ <group_ids> )
  AND agent_id == <agent_id>      # when set
  AND app_id   == <app_id>        # when set
```

`agent_id` / `app_id` AND on top of the union — they narrow it (e.g.
"the trip facts, but only this agent's"), never widen it.

So the response carries the caller's personal memories **and** all
memories tagged to those groups, from any user in the org. A
traveler's AI asking "what's the plan?" gets the traveler's own
preferences *and* the group's shared trip facts in one call — not
one or the other. Group membership (`∩`) is OR / any-of across the
list. Knowing a group id is the access boundary (ids are server-
generated unguessable handles); other users' *untagged* memories
never surface. Omit `group_ids` (or pass `[]`) to scope to the
caller's own memories only.

Each returned row reports its **actual stored** entity ids
(`user_id`, `agent_id`, `app_id`) and `group_ids` — the server
round-trips to stored metadata, so under union you can tell which
rows are the caller's own (their `user_id` matches) versus shared
from another traveler (a different `user_id`). Matches what you'd
see on `GET /v1/memories/{id}` or the list endpoint.

## Memory object

```
{
  "id":         "<uuid>",
  "object":    "memory",
  "type":      "fact" | "artifact" | "episode",
  "text":      "short readable preview",
  "user_id":   "...", "agent_id": "...", "conv_id": "...", "app_id": "...",
  "group_ids":  ["grp_..."],  // tags from the group classifier
  "categories": [...],
  "score":      0.83,         // present on search responses only
  "created_at": "2026-05-15T18:42:11Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-05-15T18:42:11Z",
  "details":    {...}         // type-specific; see FactDetails /
                              //  ArtifactDetails / EpisodeDetails schemas
}
```

`type` is the discriminator. `details` carries the per-type extension
fields; the three variants are in the components section.

## Pagination

Cursor-based on list. Cursors are opaque, tenant-scoped strings.
`has_more` plus `next_cursor` drive the next page. Default ordering
is `created_at_desc`; pass `order=created_at_asc` for the reverse.
`limit` is 1–500 (default 50). Search is not cursor-paginated — the
retrieval pipeline returns a bounded ranked set per call.

## Filtering

`GET /v1/memories` accepts flat equality on the four entity fields
(`user_id`, `agent_id`, `conv_id`, `app_id`), `group_id` (singular —
filters to rows tagged with this group), plus `type`.

`POST /v1/memories/search` is agentic: scope is enforced by the
required `user_id` field, corpora are picked via `include`
(subset of `{"fact","artifact","episode"}`), and `mode` selects
the pipeline depth:

- `compose` (default) — run vector retrieval *and* the LLM context-
  selection step that assembles a markdown prompt block. Response
  carries both `data` (the retrieval candidates as Memory rows) and
  `context` (the assembled markdown ready to drop into an LLM
  prompt). The dominant chat-app use case.
- `retrieve` — stop after vector retrieval. Cheaper, faster, no LLM
  call. Response carries `data`; `context` is null. For callers
  building their own UI or doing custom downstream processing.

`data` is **always** populated regardless of mode. Under `compose`,
`data` is the set the LLM saw before context-selection — possibly a
superset of what wound up in `context`. Useful for showing
"everything we found" alongside "what we sent to the model."

The optional `group_ids` flips scope from per-user to per-group for
the trip-sharing read path (see "Filtering by group on search") —
pass the set of groups you want to search across (OR / any-of).

`agent_id` and `app_id` are optional narrowing pins: when set they
**AND** onto whichever primary scope is active (user or group), so
`group_ids=[trip] + agent_id=bot` returns that bot's rows within the
trip. They never replace the primary scope. No other caller-supplied
filter DSL.

## Error envelope

All non-2xx responses use a single body shape:

```
{
  "detail": {
    "code":    "<stable error code>",
    "message": "<human-readable summary>"
  }
}
```

Switch on `detail.code` rather than parsing the message. Each endpoint
declares the codes it can return.

Pydantic field-validation failures (422) use the FastAPI default
shape — `detail` is an array of `{loc, msg, type}` entries listing
each invalid field.

## Response headers

- `X-Request-Id` — echo of the inbound request id, or a server-
  generated UUID. Use this when filing support requests; operator
  logs are keyed on it.
- `RateLimit-Limit`, `RateLimit-Remaining`, `RateLimit-Reset` —
  current bucket state for this `(org_id, key_hash)` pair.
- `Retry-After` — present on 429 responses, seconds until the next
  permitted request.

post.description:
Async ingest. Returns ``202 + job_id`` by default; the extraction
runs in the background and the caller polls
``GET /v1/memories/jobs/{job_id}``.

With ``?wait=true`` the server holds the connection up to ~30
seconds waiting for extraction to terminate. If it finishes in
time, the response is ``200 OK`` with the terminal job inline
(``status: "succeeded"`` or ``"failed"``). If the wait window
elapses first, the response falls back to ``202 + pending`` and
the caller resumes the normal polling pattern.

parameters.name: wait

schema.description: When true, hold the connection up to ~30s waiting for extraction to terminate. Returns 200 + terminal job inline on success; falls back to 202 + `pending` on timeout. Default false → 202 + pending immediately.
schema.title: Wait

parameters.description: When true, hold the connection up to ~30s waiting for extraction to terminate. Returns 200 + terminal job inline on success; falls back to 202 + `pending` on timeout. Default false → 202 + pending immediately.

200.description: Inline terminal response. Returned when `?wait=true` is set and the extraction pipeline completes inside the wait window (~30s). Body is the terminal `IngestJobResponse` with `status: "succeeded"` (carrying `result`) or `"failed"` (carrying `error`).

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

500.description: Server-side failure. Body carries `detail.code = "server_error"` with a stable error code; details are in the operator log, indexed by the `X-Request-Id` echoed in the response header.

get.description: List memories with flat-equality filters and cursor pagination.

parameters.name: user_id

schema.description: Filter by user_id (exact match). Combine with other entity params for AND.
schema.title: User Id

parameters.description: Filter by user_id (exact match). Combine with other entity params for AND.

parameters.name: group_id

schema.description: Filter to rows tagged with this group id. Singular on list; the full DSL on `POST /v1/memories/search` supports multi-id `$in` filtering.
schema.title: Group Id

parameters.description: Filter to rows tagged with this group id. Singular on list; the full DSL on `POST /v1/memories/search` supports multi-id `$in` filtering.

parameters.name: type

schema.description: Restrict results to one memory subtype. Default: all three.
schema.title: Type

parameters.description: Restrict results to one memory subtype. Default: all three.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's `next_cursor`. Must match this request's `order` and the issuing org (mismatch → 422 `cursor_mismatch`).
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's `next_cursor`. Must match this request's `order` and the issuing org (mismatch → 422 `cursor_mismatch`).

parameters.name: include

schema.description: Comma-separated opt-in extras. Currently only `full_content` is supported (populates `details.full_content` on artifact rows).
schema.title: Include

parameters.description: Comma-separated opt-in extras. Currently only `full_content` is supported (populates `details.full_content` on artifact rows).

422.description: Validation error — unsupported `order`, unsupported `include` option, malformed `cursor`, or `cursor` belonging to a different org / order than the request.

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

500.description: Server-side failure. Body carries `detail.code = "server_error"` with a stable error code; details are in the operator log, indexed by the `X-Request-Id` echoed in the response header.

get.description:
Return the current state of an ingest job.

Terminal states (``succeeded`` / ``failed``) remain queryable
after the job completes. ``result.memories_created`` and
``result.memories_updated`` carry thin references
(``{id, type, text}``); fetch ``GET /v1/memories/{id}`` for the
full row. Returns ``404 job_not_found`` for unknown ids or ids
belonging to a different org.

404.description: `detail.code = "job_not_found"` — unknown job id or tenant mismatch.

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

500.description: Server-side failure. Body carries `detail.code = "server_error"` with a stable error code; details are in the operator log, indexed by the `X-Request-Id` echoed in the response header.

post.description:
Agentic search over the memory pool.

Pipeline: per-corpus vector retrieval → (``mode=compose`` only)
LLM context selection. ``data: list[Memory]`` is always returned;
``mode=compose`` additionally assembles a markdown block into
``context: str``, while ``mode=retrieve`` stops after retrieval
and leaves ``context`` null. Scope is enforced server-side from the
request's scope axes (``user_id`` / ``group_ids`` / ``agent_id`` /
``app_id``); no caller-supplied filter DSL.

422.description: Validation error — invalid query, user_id, mode, or include.

500.description: `detail.code = "search_failed"` — store init, retrieval, or row-build failure.

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

post.description:
Procedural-memory recall for a pre-tool-call hook.

Fires the symbol tripwire on the in-flight ``action`` (or explicit ``entities``)
and returns the ``lesson``/``procedure`` insights past sessions recorded about
those symbols — advisory, not a mandate. ``data: list[Memory]`` carries the matched
rows (``type: "lesson" | "procedure"``); ``mode=compose`` additionally runs an LLM
relevance gate over ``task`` and assembles a markdown block into ``context``, while
``mode=retrieve`` returns the raw matched rows and leaves ``context`` null. Scope is
enforced server-side from the request's scope axes (``user_id`` / ``group_ids`` /
``agent_id`` / ``app_id``).

Unlike ``POST /v1/memories/search``, this endpoint is **not** metered against the
monthly quota — the hook is meant to be called freely before every tool use.

422.description: Validation error — missing firing signal (`action`/`entities`), no scope axis, or invalid `include`/`mode`.

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

get.description:
Return the revision chain for a memory.

Facts → supersede chain (oldest → newest).
Artifacts → version chain (v1 → vN).
Episodes → single-element list (episodes have no revisions today).

Full chain in one response. ``has_more`` is always ``false``;
cursor envelope kept for shape consistency.

404.description: `detail.code = "memory_not_found"` — no row with that id under this org.

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

500.description: Server-side failure. Body carries `detail.code = "server_error"` with a stable error code; details are in the operator log, indexed by the `X-Request-Id` echoed in the response header.

get.description:
Get one memory by id. Works for facts, artifacts, episodes.
Always returns the full representation — artifacts include
``details.full_content``.

404.description: `detail.code = "memory_not_found"` — no row with that id under this org.

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

500.description: Server-side failure. Body carries `detail.code = "server_error"` with a stable error code; details are in the operator log, indexed by the `X-Request-Id` echoed in the response header.

patch.description:
Add or remove ``group_ids`` on a single memory — the sharing
axis. Group tags control who can reach a row via
``filters: {group_ids: <id>}`` on search / list, so this is how a
memory becomes shared (or un-shared) after ingest.

Scope is group tags only: ``text`` and the entity ids
(``user_id`` / ``agent_id`` / ``conv_id`` / ``app_id``) are
immutable post-ingest and are not editable here. The operation is
set-based and idempotent — re-sending the same patch is a no-op and
skips the write entirely. See :class:`MemoryGroupPatchRequest` for
the full contract.

200.description: Updated. Returns the full memory with its new `group_ids`.

404.description: `detail.code = "memory_not_found"` — no readable row with that id under this org.

422.description: `detail.code` is one of: `empty_patch` (neither list supplied), `contradictory_group_ids` (same id in both lists), `invalid_group_ids` (an added id is unknown or archived), or `invalid_request` (resulting tag count exceeds `MAX_GROUP_IDS_PER_INGEST`).

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

delete.description:
Hard-delete a memory — fact, artifact, or episode.

Removes the record outright (no soft-delete / tombstone). A
deleted fact disappears from supersede chains; the revision walker
(``GET /{id}/revisions``) tolerates the missing node and simply
stops there. Idempotent: the first delete returns 204, subsequent
deletes return 404 (the record is gone, so the existence check
below fails).

401.description: Authentication failed. Missing / invalid API key. Body carries `detail.code = "unauthorized"`.

429.description: Rate-limit or daily-cap exceeded. `Retry-After` and `RateLimit-*` response headers indicate when to retry.

get.description:
Aggregate the org's memory usage for the current quota period — a
calendar month, or your billing period on plans that meter per billing
period — plus a per-day breakdown if requested, plus an inline storage
snapshot.

- ``operations`` totals ``messages_ingested`` / ``searches`` /
  ``requests`` across every API key for the period.
- ``quota.monthly`` pairs those totals against the plan caps
  resolved from the org's subscription tier; ``limit: null`` means
  the tier is uncapped (enterprise).
- ``quota.rate_limit_req_per_min`` is the per-API-key request
  ceiling enforced across all memory endpoints.

parameters.name: daily

schema.description: Include the per-day ``daily[]`` array (one entry per calendar day in the period). Off by default to keep the response cheap; the dashboard chart opts in.
schema.title: Daily

parameters.description: Include the per-day ``daily[]`` array (one entry per calendar day in the period). Off by default to keep the response cheap; the dashboard chart opts in.

parameters.name: by_api_key

schema.description: Include the per-API-key breakdown with each key's ``name`` / ``first_n`` display fields. Off by default — adds extra lookups.
schema.title: By Api Key

parameters.description: Include the per-API-key breakdown with each key's ``name`` / ``first_n`` display fields. Off by default — adds extra lookups.

post.description:
Register a new group on the org. Returns the persisted row
including the server-generated `id`.

get.description: List groups for the calling org. Active-only by default.

parameters.name: include_archived

schema.description: When true, the response includes archived groups in addition to active ones. Default false — most callers only need the currently-taggable set.
schema.title: Include Archived

parameters.description: When true, the response includes archived groups in addition to active ones. Default false — most callers only need the currently-taggable set.

404.description: `detail.code = "group_not_found"` — unknown id or id belonging to a different org.

patch.description:
Patch `name`, `prompt`, and/or `status`. Changing `prompt` does
not retroactively re-tag memory rows that were tagged under the
previous prompt.

422.description: `detail.code = "invalid_request"` — e.g. `status` set to a value other than `active` / `archived`.

delete.description:
Soft-delete: flips `status` to `archived`. The group row is
retained so memory rows tagged with this id remain searchable;
new ingests reject the id with `422 group_archived`. Restore by
`PATCH`ing `status` back to `active`. Idempotent.

put.description:
Create or replace the org's webhook config. Idempotent on `url` /
`events` / `enabled`. The full signing `secret` is returned here —
on first create, or whenever `rotate_secret=true`. Store it: `GET`
only ever returns it masked.

parameters.name: rotate_secret

schema.description: When true, mint a fresh signing secret even if a config already exists. Default false preserves the existing secret so an URL/events edit doesn't break the subscriber.
schema.title: Rotate Secret

parameters.description: When true, mint a fresh signing secret even if a config already exists. Default false preserves the existing secret so an URL/events edit doesn't break the subscriber.

422.description: `detail.code = "invalid_webhook_url"` — URL is malformed, not https, or resolves to a non-public address. Or `"invalid_request"` — empty `events`.

get.description:
Read the org's current webhook config. The `secret` is masked —
the full value is only ever shown at create / rotate time.

404.description: `detail.code = "webhook_not_found"` — no config set for this org.

delete.description:
Remove the org's webhook config. Idempotent — returns `204`
whether or not a config existed. Stops all deliveries for the org.

tool.title: Tool
tool.description: Tool / MCP name about to run (e.g. "Edit", "linode_api").

output.title: Output
output.description: Most-recent tool output, if firing post-call.

ActionContext.title: ActionContext
ActionContext.description:
The in-flight tool call the agent is touching — the firing signal for procedural
recall on ``POST /v1/memories/trigger``. The server extracts greppable identifiers
from ``tool`` / ``args`` / ``output`` and matches them
exactly against directives' ``trigger_entities``. At a pre-tool-call hook the
high-value signal is ``tool`` + ``args`` (the intended call); ``output`` is for
firing off a just-returned result.

first_n.title: First N
first_n.description: Display prefix of the API key.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable label for the API key.

last_used_at.title: Last Used At
last_used_at.description: Most recent ``updatedAt`` across this key's memory rollup rows for the period. ``None`` until the key has any usage.

DailyEntry.title: DailyEntry
DailyEntry.description: One row in the ``daily[]`` array — chart x-axis points.

code.title: Code
code.description: Stable error identifier. Switch on this rather than parsing the message. Common values: `invalid_request`, `invalid_messages`, `memory_not_found`, `job_not_found`, `immutable_field`, `reserved_field`, `empty_text_field`, `missing_user_id`, `missing_conv_id`, `unsupported_include_option`, `cursor_mismatch`, `unauthorized`, `forbidden`, `rate_limited`, `delete_failed`, `search_failed`, `ingest_failed`, `server_error`.

message.title: Message
message.description: Human-readable summary; safe to log but not safe to switch on.

ErrorDetail.title: ErrorDetail
ErrorDetail.description:
Inner ``detail`` block on every non-2xx response raised by a
memory-API route handler. ``code`` is the stable identifier
clients should switch on; ``message`` is a sanitized human-
readable summary.

ErrorEnvelope.title: ErrorEnvelope
ErrorEnvelope.description:
Standard error body for non-2xx responses raised by the memory
API. Pydantic field-validation failures (422) use the FastAPI-
default ``HTTPValidationError`` shape instead, where ``detail`` is
an array of per-field error entries — switch on the response
status code to pick the right shape.

object.title: Object
object.description: Constant discriminator for the resource type.

id.title: Id
id.description: Stable group id of the form `grp_<32-hex-chars>`.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Classifier criterion. Read by the ingest pipeline on every request that includes this group's id in `group_ids`. `null` marks a catch-all group: every extracted memory judged shareable (not personal) is tagged with it.

status.title: Status
status.description: `active` groups are tagged on new ingests. `archived` groups still surface on search (rows tagged with the id stay reachable) but the ingest classifier rejects them.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the group was registered.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

Group.title: Group
Group.description:
Wire shape of a group. ``id`` is the opaque handle that goes
into ``IngestRequest.group_ids`` and into search ``filters``.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable label, shown in UI. Not used by the classifier.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Free-text criterion the ingest classifier reads to decide whether an extracted memory belongs in this group. Describe what belongs, not how the classifier should behave. Omit (or send null) to make the group a catch-all: every extracted memory judged shareable (not personal) is tagged with it, with no per-group matching.
examples: Facts about the Tokyo trip in May 2026: dates, flights, hotels, restaurants, dietary preferences for this trip.

GroupCreateRequest.title: GroupCreateRequest
GroupCreateRequest.description:
POST /v1/groups — register a new group.

The ``prompt`` is the instruction the ingest classifier reads on
every ingest to decide whether an extracted memory belongs in this
group. Write it as a description of *what* belongs, not a chat
instruction — e.g. "Facts about the Tokyo trip: dates, flights,
hotels, dietary preferences for Tokyo", not "tag this if you think
it should be tagged".

Omit ``prompt`` (or send ``null``) to create a catch-all group:
every extracted memory the classifier judges shareable (not
personal) is tagged with it — no per-group matching. Sending an
empty string is still a 422; only omission/null means catch-all,
so a blank field from a buggy client can't silently create one.

object.title: Object
object.description: Constant discriminator for the resource type.

data.title: Data
data.description: All matching groups in the org.

GroupListEnvelope.title: GroupListEnvelope
GroupListEnvelope.description:
Stripe-style list envelope. No cursor for now — group count is
bounded by ``Settings.MAX_GROUPS_PER_ORG`` (default 100) so a
single page is always sufficient.

name.title: Name
name.description: New name; leave null to keep existing.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: New classifier prompt; leave null to keep existing. Does not retroactively re-tag existing memory rows. Setting a prompt on a catch-all group converts it to a prompted group going forward; a prompt can never be removed via PATCH (empty string is a 422).

status.title: Status
status.description: Set to `"active"` to un-archive a previously-archived group. The normal way to archive is `DELETE /v1/groups/{id}`.

GroupUpdateRequest.title: GroupUpdateRequest
GroupUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH /v1/groups/{id} — partial update. Any field set to ``None``
is left unchanged. Editing ``prompt`` does NOT retroactively re-tag
existing memory rows.

type.title: Type
type.description: Error class — typically `"server_error"` for ingest failures.

code.title: Code
code.description: Stable error code; switch on this rather than the message. Typically `ingest_failed`.

message.title: Message
message.description: Human-readable, sanitized summary. Safe to log; not safe to switch on.

IngestJobError.title: IngestJobError
IngestJobError.description:
Job-level error payload. Mirrors the global error envelope
shape so clients can branch on ``code`` the same way they do for
direct request failures.

object.title: Object
object.description: Constant discriminator for the resource type.

id.title: Id
id.description: Opaque job id of the form `job_<32-hex-chars>`.

status.title: Status
status.description: Lifecycle state. `pending` → enqueued. `running` → extraction in progress. `succeeded` → `result` is set. `failed` → `error` is set. Terminal jobs (`succeeded` / `failed`) are retained for 24h before TTL sweep returns 404 `job_not_found`.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the job was created.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent state transition.

IngestJobResponse.title: IngestJobResponse
IngestJobResponse.description:
Returned by ``POST /v1/memories`` (with status 202 by default,
200 when ``?wait=true`` succeeds inline) and by
``GET /v1/memories/jobs/{job_id}``. Same wire shape across
every read of a job's lifecycle.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Chat-style turns to extract memories from. Must be non-empty — an empty list returns 400 `invalid_messages` (the route raises this explicitly so the wire error carries the stable code rather than a generic Pydantic validation message). Server picks live vs batch extraction by length; no client-facing strategy hint.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User identifier. REQUIRED. Keys the per-user session namespace so a user's facts accumulate across their conversations rather than fragmenting per-conv. Also stored as an indexed filter axis on every row.

conv_id.title: Conv Id
conv_id.description: Conversation identifier. REQUIRED. Anchors every extracted memory to a conversation for replay, export, and bulk retract.

agent_id.title: Agent Id
agent_id.description: Optional agent scope. Indexed alongside the other entity ids.

app_id.title: App Id
app_id.description: Optional app scope. Indexed alongside the other entity ids.

timestamp_format.title: Timestamp Format
timestamp_format.description: Optional `strptime` format string used to parse each message's `date` field into a real timestamp on the batch-extraction path (e.g. `"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"`). When omitted, or when a `date` value fails to parse, the turn keeps its raw date string with no parsed timestamp. Only consulted on the batch path (message count above the live threshold); the live path ignores it.

extract_artifacts.title: Extract Artifacts
extract_artifacts.description: When true, extracted artifacts are also stored and returned as `artifact` memories, in addition to fact + episode extraction. Off by default — most expensive stage and most callers don't need it. Routing (live vs batch) is unaffected and still chosen by message count.

agentic.title: Agentic
agentic.description: When true, ingest through the agentic (task-aware) path: recall-first extraction + a Stage-2 noise filter + agentic artifact detection + a structured session gist. Intended for tool-using / coding-agent transcripts. Off by default (conversational extraction).

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: Namespace this session belongs to — the working context, e.g. a coding agent's repo, a support agent's customer account, an ops agent's service. Captured `lesson`/`procedure` directives are written under it, so recall with the same `namespace` (see `POST /v1/memories/trigger`) surfaces them there instead of in every context. Directives ingested without one are global. Unrelated to the tenancy axes (`user_id`/`agent_id`/`group_ids`).

group_ids.title: Group Ids
group_ids.description: Optional list of group ids the client wants associated with this ingest. Each id must be a registered group on the org (see `POST /v1/groups`). Ids that are unknown or archived are dropped silently and echoed back in `result.ignored_group_ids` — a single stale id will not fail the ingest, since the chat path tolerates partial tagging better than a hard reject. After extraction, a classifier judges each memory personal-vs-shareable: personal memories are never group-tagged (they stay in the author's personal scope); shareable memories are tagged with every prompt-less (catch-all) group in the valid subset, plus whichever prompted groups the classifier matched. Tagged rows become reachable via `filters: {group_ids: <id>}` on search. The capped length is `MAX_GROUP_IDS_PER_INGEST`.

IngestRequest.title: IngestRequest
IngestRequest.description:
POST /v1/memories — async ingest from a message list.

Required entities: ``user_id`` and ``conv_id``. ``user_id`` scopes
a user's per-user memory namespace, so a user's facts accumulate in
one place rather than fragmenting per-conversation. ``conv_id``
anchors every extracted memory to a conversation for replay, export,
and bulk retract. ``agent_id`` and ``app_id`` remain optional — set
either, both, or neither. There is no auto-default ``app_id`` rule.

``extract_artifacts`` opts in to the artifact-extraction stage
(off by default — the most expensive stage and most callers don't
need it). Episodes are always extracted (``conv_id`` is
guaranteed). Facts are always extracted.

There is no client-facing pipeline hint: the server selects its
extraction strategy automatically based on the number of messages.

object.title: Object
object.description: Constant discriminator for the resource type.

memories_created.title: Memories Created
memories_created.description: Thin references to new facts / episodes / artifacts written this ingest. Each entry is `{id, type, text}`; full row shape is at `GET /v1/memories/{id}`.

memories_updated.title: Memories Updated
memories_updated.description: Thin references to existing rows whose payload was updated during this ingest (e.g. artifact dedup hits).

memories_superseded_by.title: Memories Superseded By
memories_superseded_by.description: Map of `old_fact_id → new_fact_id`. Populated when the extraction pipeline detects a contradiction with an existing active fact: the old fact's `tag2` flips to superseded and the new fact records `supersedes = old_id`.

ignored_group_ids.title: Ignored Group Ids
ignored_group_ids.description: Subset of the request's `group_ids` that the server dropped because the id was unknown or archived. Empty on the common path. Lets clients self-correct stale caches without having to re-issue the ingest.

IngestResult.title: IngestResult
IngestResult.description:
Terminal job ``result`` payload, populated when status is
``succeeded``. ``memories_created`` and ``memories_updated`` carry
thin :class:`MemoryRef` entries rather than full ``Memory`` rows
— see :class:`MemoryRef` for the trade-off rationale and how to
fetch the full row when needed.

object.title: Object
object.description: Constant discriminator for the resource type.

has_more.title: Has More
has_more.description: True if more rows exist beyond this page; use `next_cursor` to fetch them.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Null on the final page. Tenant-scoped: only usable with the same `(org, key)` that produced it.

ListEnvelope.title: ListEnvelope
ListEnvelope.description:
Stripe-style list envelope used by ``GET /v1/memories``,
``GET /v1/memories/{id}/revisions``, and the default search
response.

id.title: Id
id.description: Stable UUID for this memory row.

object.title: Object
object.description: Constant discriminator for the resource type.

type.title: Type
type.description: Memory subtype. `fact` = a single semantic claim extracted from a turn; `artifact` = a structured object (code, doc, image) referenced by the conversation; `episode` = a session-scoped summary of a stretch of turns; `lesson` / `procedure` = a situated directive recalled by the symbol tripwire (see `DirectiveDetails`).

text.title: Text
text.description: Short readable preview. For facts: the claim statement. For artifacts: a summary or title (full body lives in `details.full_content`, opt-in via `include=full_content`). For episodes: a summary of the session.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User scope this row belongs to.

group_ids.title: Group Ids
group_ids.description: Group ids associated with this row — the sharing axis. Stamped at ingest time and editable afterward via `PATCH /v1/memories/{id}` (`add_group_ids` / `remove_group_ids`). Reachable as a filter axis via `filters: {group_ids: <id>}` or `{group_ids: {"$in": [<id>, ...]}}`.

categories.title: Categories
categories.description: Optional category labels from the extraction pipeline.

score.title: Score
score.description: Vector-similarity score. Present **only** on search responses; null on list / get-by-id / patch / ingest result rows.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last supersede / consolidation.

details.title: Details
details.description: Type-specific extension. Shape depends on `type` — see `FactDetails` / `ArtifactDetails` / `EpisodeDetails` schemas. Always an object; never null.

Memory.title: Memory
Memory.description:
Unified memory resource — facts, artifacts, episodes marshal to
this shape. ``type`` is the discriminator; ``details`` is the
type-specific extension.

Invariant: ``text`` is always a short readable preview, regardless
of type (fact statement / artifact summary / episode summary).

add_group_ids.title: Add Group Ids
add_group_ids.description: Group ids to add to the row. Each must be an active group on the org. Ids already present are ignored (no duplicates). Blank entries are trimmed away.

remove_group_ids.title: Remove Group Ids
remove_group_ids.description: Group ids to remove from the row. Ids not currently on the row are ignored. Not validated against the group registry, so an archived group's tag can still be removed.

MemoryGroupPatchRequest.title: MemoryGroupPatchRequest
MemoryGroupPatchRequest.description:
``PATCH /v1/memories/{id}`` — add or remove ``group_ids`` on an
existing memory row.

Scope is intentionally narrow: this endpoint mutates the row's
group tags only — the *sharing* axis. It does not edit ``text`` or
any entity id (``user_id`` / ``agent_id`` / ``conv_id`` /
``app_id``); those are immutable once ingested.

Semantics are set-based and order-independent — ``group_ids`` is an
unordered set of tags. Adding an id already present is a no-op, and
removing an id that isn't there is a no-op, so the whole operation
is idempotent and safe to retry. ``remove`` is applied before
``add``; the same id may not appear in both lists (``422
contradictory_group_ids``). At least one list must be non-empty
(``422 empty_patch``).

Every id in ``add_group_ids`` must be an **active** group on the
org (see ``POST /v1/groups``); an unknown or archived id is
rejected with ``422 invalid_group_ids``. ``remove_group_ids`` is
*not* validated against the registry — a tag can always be removed,
including one whose group was archived after it was applied.

period_key.title: Period Key
period_key.description: Identifier for the current quota period — ``YYYY-MM`` for a calendar month, or the period's start date ``YYYY-MM-DD`` for a billing period.

resets_at.title: Resets At
resets_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC instant when the quota counters reset (the start of the next calendar month, or the end of the current billing period).

MemoryMonthlyQuota.title: MemoryMonthlyQuota
MemoryMonthlyQuota.description:
Quota state for the current period — a calendar month, or your billing
period on plans that meter per billing period.

id.title: Id
id.description: Stable UUID of the written / updated row.

type.title: Type
type.description: Memory subtype. Determines which detail variant lives behind the id when fetched via `GET /v1/memories/{id}`.

text.title: Text
text.description: Short readable preview as captured at write time. Same value as ``Memory.text`` on the row at the moment of ingest. **Snapshot, not live state** — a subsequent supersede (text PATCH) replaces the row's current text; the value here keeps reflecting what the ingest job wrote. Fetch ``GET /v1/memories/{id}`` for current state.

MemoryRef.title: MemoryRef
MemoryRef.description:
Thin reference to a memory row written or updated by an ingest
job. Carries only what a client needs for confirmation / per-type
routing; the full ``Memory`` shape (entity ids, details,
timestamps, categories) is one ``GET /v1/memories/{id}`` away.

Trade-off rationale: storing the full ``Memory`` shape inside the
job record duplicates content that is already persisted — every
field except ``id`` is derivable via the read endpoints. The thin
shape keeps the record small while preserving the two fields that
make a poll response useful on its own: ``type``
(lets clients route per memory subtype without a follow-up) and
``text`` (the human-readable preview, makes the result usable as
a confirmation receipt).

content.title: Content
examples: I like Thai food and spicy dishes.

date.title: Date
date.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp for the turn. Used by the batch ingest path when provided; ignored by the live path.

dia_id.title: Dia Id
dia_id.description: Optional client-supplied per-turn id (carried for trace/eval pipelines).

Message.title: Message
Message.description:
One chat-style turn in an ingest payload. Order matters: facts
and episodes are extracted by walking adjacent user→assistant
pairs (the live path) or the full sequence (the batch path).

messages_ingested.title: Messages Ingested
messages_ingested.description: Chat messages ingested via POST /v1/memories — the sum of payload message counts, not request count and not the memories extracted from them.

requests.title: Requests
requests.description: Total metered memory API calls (ingest + search).

Operations.title: Operations
Operations.description: Period-total memory operation counters for the org.

key.title: Key
key.description: Period identifier — ``YYYY-MM`` for ``calendar_month``, or the period's start date ``YYYY-MM-DD`` for ``billing_period``.

rate_limit_req_per_min.title: Rate Limit Req Per Min
rate_limit_req_per_min.description: Per-API-key request ceiling across all memory endpoints.

used.title: Used
used.description: Consumed so far in the current quota period.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Cap for the org's plan; ``null`` = unlimited (enterprise).

QuotaCounter.title: QuotaCounter
QuotaCounter.description: One metered quota dimension: amount used vs the plan cap.

query.title: Query
query.description: Natural-language query text. Embedded server-side; the search pipeline ranks and selects.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: Scope key. When supplied, baked into the per-request store as an AND pin so reads are tenant-isolated at the vector-store filter layer. **Optional** on search (unlike ingest): omit it and pass ``group_ids`` to read a shared group across users. At least one of ``user_id`` / ``agent_id`` / ``app_id`` / ``group_ids`` is required. The compat shim also accepts it inside legacy ``filters.user_id`` and lifts it before field validation runs, so old SDKs that send the pre-#68 wire shape don't 422.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description:
Pipeline depth selector.

- `compose` (default) — vector retrieval **plus** an LLM context-selection step that picks the most relevant subset, then assembles it into a markdown block. `data` carries the selected rows; `context` carries the assembled markdown. One LLM call.
- `retrieve` — vector retrieval only. No LLM, no agent, cheaper and faster. `data` carries the raw ranked candidates (the unfiltered set); `context` is null.

`data` is populated in both modes; under `compose` it's the LLM-selected subset, under `retrieve` it's the raw candidate set.

include.title: Include
include.description: Which corpora to search. Subset to restrict — e.g. `["fact"]` for facts-only. Default is all three.

group_ids.title: Group Ids
group_ids.description:
Optional group tags — another AND scope axis. When non-empty, candidate rows must be tagged to at least one of the requested group(s):

  `org [ AND user_id ] AND ( group_ids ∩ <group_ids> ) [ AND agent_id ] [ AND app_id ]`

Group membership (the `∩`) is OR / any-of across the list — pass `[trip_tokyo, trip_paris]` to span both trips.

How it composes with `user_id` ("scope by what you pass"):

- **omit `user_id`, pass `group_ids`** — the cross-user whole-group read: every user's rows tagged to those group(s). A traveler's AI sees the whole trip's shared facts, from any traveler.
- **pass both `user_id` and `group_ids`** — the intersection: only the caller's own rows that are also tagged to those group(s) (the caller's slice of the group).

(`agent_id` / `app_id` AND on top when set — they narrow further.) Group ids are server-generated unguessable handles (see `POST /v1/groups`), so knowing the id is the access boundary; other users' *untagged* memories never surface.

agent_id.title: Agent Id
agent_id.description: Optional agent scope. When set, ANDs onto the active primary scope (whether that's `user_id` or `group_ids`): candidate rows must also carry this exact `agent_id`. Use it to narrow a search to one agent's contributions. Indexed payload key, same axis ingest stamps.

app_id.title: App Id
app_id.description: Optional app scope. When set, ANDs onto the active primary scope (like `agent_id`): candidate rows must also carry this exact `app_id`. Indexed payload key, same axis ingest stamps.

SearchRequest.title: SearchRequest
SearchRequest.description:
POST /v1/memories/search — agentic memory search.

The pipeline has two phases:

1. **Retrieve** — embed the query, fetch per-corpus vector
   candidates, optionally rerank. Output: a ranked set of
   :class:`Memory` rows.
2. **Compose** *(only when ``mode='compose'``)* — an LLM
   context-selection step picks the most relevant subset of the
   candidates and weaves them into a markdown block ready to drop
   into an LLM prompt.

``mode`` toggles whether phase 2 runs:

- ``"compose"`` (default) → ``data`` populated with rows **and**
  ``context`` populated with the assembled markdown. The dominant
  use case (memory search → LLM prompt) gets the prompt-ready
  blob without an opt-in.
- ``"retrieve"`` → ``data`` populated, ``context`` null. Skips
  the LLM compose step (cheaper, faster); for callers building
  their own UI or doing custom downstream processing.

Scope is enforced server-side by the per-request store and
follows "scope by what you
pass": every scope axis you supply ANDs; an axis you omit is left
unconstrained. ``org_id`` is derived server-side from your API key. At least one of
``user_id`` / ``agent_id`` / ``app_id`` / ``group_ids`` must be
supplied — an unscoped org-wide search is rejected. ``user_id`` is
optional here (unlike ingest, where it is required): omit it and
pass ``group_ids`` to read a shared group across users. No filter
DSL — the four scope axes are the only narrowing.

**Legacy compatibility** (undocumented in the public spec, kept
so existing SDK installations don't break):

- ``filters: {user_id: "X", ...}`` — accepted; ``user_id`` is
  lifted out of ``filters`` if absent at the body root. Other
  filter keys are silently dropped (the new shape doesn't support
  a general filter DSL).
- ``mode: "rows"`` → translated to ``"retrieve"``.
- ``mode: "context"`` → translated to ``"compose"``.
- ``include: ["context_prompt"]`` → sets ``mode="compose"`` and
  drops the value. ``"full_content"`` is dropped silently.

These translations are intentionally invisible on the wire — the
public spec only advertises the new shape — and will be removed
once consumers have migrated.

object.title: Object
object.description: Constant discriminator for the resource type.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Echoes the request's mode. `compose` ⇒ both `data` and `context` populated; `retrieve` ⇒ `data` populated and `context` null.

data.title: Data
data.description: Ranked Memory rows from retrieval. Populated for both modes. Semantic rows (`fact`/`artifact`/`episode`) are ordered by `score` desc; when `include` requests `lesson`/`procedure`, those directive rows are precision-gated (not vector-scored, `score` is null) and lead the list ahead of the semantic rows. For `mode=compose`, the semantic rows are the candidates considered by the context-selection step — a (possibly larger) superset of what ended up in `context`.

context.title: Context
context.description: Assembled markdown context block ready for prompt insertion. Populated only when `mode=compose`; null otherwise.

stage_timings.title: Stage Timings
stage_timings.description: Per-stage retrieval-pipeline latencies (seconds). Populated in both modes.

context_selection_applied.title: Context Selection Applied
context_selection_applied.description: True when the LLM context-selection step ran. False indicates the pipeline fell through (e.g. an empty candidate pool, or context selection disabled by config).

SearchResponse.title: SearchResponse
SearchResponse.description:
POST /v1/memories/search response.

``data`` is **always** populated — both modes return the ranked
Memory rows from retrieval. ``context`` is populated **only when
``mode='compose'``** with the assembled markdown block from the
LLM context-selection step. ``stage_timings`` is always present
for per-stage latency attribution.

Note that under ``mode='compose'`` the rows in ``data`` are the
**retrieval candidates** — a possibly larger set than the subset
the LLM selected and wove into ``context``. Useful for showing
"everything we found" alongside "what we sent to the model."

StorageSnapshot.title: StorageSnapshot
StorageSnapshot.description:
Currently-active per-type row counts.

Cached harder than operations (computing the counts is expensive);
``as_of`` reflects the mint time of the cached snapshot and may
lag the response-level ``as_of``.

action.description: The in-flight tool call the agent is touching right now — the firing signal for recall. Greppable identifiers are extracted server-side (`tool` name, `args` values, optional `output`) and matched exactly against directives' `trigger_entities`. Supply this or `entities`.

entities.title: Entities
entities.description: Escape hatch: pre-extracted greppable identifiers to fire on, bypassing server-side extraction from `action`. Use when the client already knows the exact symbols the agent is touching.

include.title: Include
include.description: Which procedural corpora to recall. Defaults to both (`lesson`/`procedure`); subset to restrict (e.g. `["lesson"]`).

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: Namespace the agent is working in right now — repo, customer account, service (whatever was passed at ingest). Narrows recall to directives learned under that namespace **plus global ones** (ingested with no namespace) — it never hides global directives, so it is always safe to pass. Unrelated to the tenancy axes.

task.title: Task
task.description: The agent's current goal, in one line. Fed to the relevance gate under `mode=compose` so it keeps only the insights that help with THIS task.
examples: fix finalize-on-abort in the tool loop

mode.title: Mode
mode.description:
Pipeline depth selector.

- `compose` (default) — symbol-tripwire recall **plus** an LLM relevance-gate step that keeps only the insights relevant to `task`, then assembles a markdown block. `data` carries the gated rows; `context` carries the markdown. One LLM call.
- `retrieve` — recall only. No LLM, cheaper and faster. `data` carries the matched rows; `context` is null.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: Scope key. When supplied, applied as an AND pin so reads are tenant-isolated at the storage filter layer. Optional: omit it and pass `group_ids` to read a shared group across users. At least one of `user_id` / `agent_id` / `app_id` / `group_ids` is required.

group_ids.title: Group Ids
group_ids.description: Optional group tags — another AND scope axis. When non-empty, matched rows must be tagged to at least one of the requested group(s). Group ids are server-generated unguessable handles (see `POST /v1/groups`); knowing the id is the access boundary.

agent_id.title: Agent Id
agent_id.description: Optional agent scope. When set, ANDs onto the active primary scope (whether that's `user_id` or `group_ids`): matched rows must also carry this exact `agent_id`.

app_id.title: App Id
app_id.description: Optional app scope. When set, ANDs onto the active primary scope (like `agent_id`): matched rows must also carry this exact `app_id`.

TriggerRequest.title: TriggerRequest
TriggerRequest.description:
POST /v1/memories/trigger — procedural-memory recall for a pre-tool-call hook.

An agent calls this right before (or just after) a tool runs to pull the
``lesson``/``procedure`` insights that past sessions recorded about the symbols
it's touching — what worked, what didn't. Recall fires on the symbol tripwire,
never a semantic query: the server extracts greppable identifiers from ``action``
(``tool`` / ``args`` / ``output``) or takes them
straight from ``entities``, and matches them exactly against directives'
``trigger_entities``.

What comes back is advisory, not a mandate — ranked :class:`Memory` rows
(``type: "lesson" | "procedure"``) in the shared :class:`SearchResponse`, with the
assembled markdown in ``context`` under ``mode="compose"``.

Scope follows "scope by what you pass": every axis you supply ANDs; ``org_id``
is derived server-side from your API key. At least one of ``user_id`` / ``agent_id`` / ``app_id`` /
``group_ids`` is required — an unscoped org-wide recall is rejected.

tier.title: Tier
tier.description: The org's subscription tier; ``None`` if unset.

object.title: Object
object.description: Constant discriminator for the resource type.

secret.title: Secret
secret.description: Signing secret used to verify `X-Webhook-Signature`. Returned in **full only when freshly minted** — a first `PUT` (create) or `PUT?rotate_secret=true`. Store it then; every other response (a plain edit, or `GET`) masks it.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the config was first created.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

WebhookConfig.title: WebhookConfig
WebhookConfig.description: Wire shape of the org's webhook config.

url.title: Url
url.description: HTTPS endpoint Xtrace POSTs terminal ingest events to. Must resolve to a public address — private / loopback / link-local hosts are rejected.

events.title: Events
events.description: Event types to receive. Omit (or null) to subscribe to all events. An empty list is rejected.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: When false, the config is stored but no events are delivered. Lets you pause delivery without losing the URL.

WebhookConfigRequest.title: WebhookConfigRequest
WebhookConfigRequest.description: ``PUT /v1/webhooks`` — set or replace the org's webhook config.

FactDetails.description:
Per-row fact details — sits under ``Memory.details`` when
``Memory.type == "fact"``.
FactDetails.title: FactDetails

ArtifactDetails.description:
Per-row artifact details under ``Memory.details`` when
``Memory.type == "artifact"``.

``full_content`` is opt-in — omitted on list/search by default,
included on ``GET /v1/memories/{id}`` and when
``include=["full_content"]`` is set on list/search.
ArtifactDetails.title: ArtifactDetails

DirectiveDetails.description:
Per-row directive details under ``Memory.details`` when ``Memory.type`` is
``"lesson"`` or ``"procedure"`` — a directive recalled by the symbol tripwire.

Populated from the recalled ``Fact`` plus the relevance gate. ``because`` /
``confidence`` are filled only under ``mode=compose`` (the gate ran); under
``mode=retrieve`` they are null (deterministic stage-1 tripwire only).

trigger_entities.description: The concrete file/symbol anchors this directive fires on.
trigger_entities.title: Trigger Entities

matched_on.description: The subset of `trigger_entities` the in-flight action actually matched.
matched_on.title: Matched On

because.description: Why the gate kept this directive for the current task. Null under `mode=retrieve`.
because.title: Because

observation_count.description: How many times this directive has been re-confirmed.
observation_count.title: Observation Count

steps.description: Typed procedure steps, in order (`type: "procedure"` only). The row's `text` already renders them as a numbered list; this is the structured form for callers that format their own.
steps.title: Steps

DirectiveDetails.title: DirectiveDetails

ApiKeyHeader.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyHeader.description: Long-lived org API key. Alternative: `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

BearerToken.description: Long-lived API key sent as `Authorization: Bearer <api-key>`.
